prompt: Explain what a "closure" is in programming, in exactly one sentence. The sentence can be long.
response: A closure is a first-class function with a purpose-specific link to the exact state (lexical environment or non-local variables) in which it was created, which remains bound for the entire life of the closure, even when the function is passed around, allowing it to be called later with access to the same state, effectively mimicking traditional class-based object-oriented private variables and methods.
